symptoms.symptom_name: Acute bilateral conjunctival hyperaemia with profuse watery discharge, one-eye-then-both spread

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Watery (serous) discharge with diffuse hyperaemia and sequential eye involvement is the hallmark of viral conjunctivitis.
Bacterial conjunctivitis typically produces thick mucopurulent (yellow-green) discharge rather than watery discharge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gritty foreign-body sensation with morning crusting and mild eyelid oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grittiness/foreign-body sensation and light morning crusting are typical of surface (viral) conjunctivitis.
Allergic conjunctivitis is dominated by intense itch rather than a gritty foreign-body sensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding upper respiratory tract infection (sore throat, coryza) ~3 days earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adenovirus causes both URI and conjunctivitis; a recent viral URI strongly supports adenoviral conjunctivitis.
An infective viral prodrome is not a feature of allergic conjunctivitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative sight-threatening red-eye red-flag screen (no severe deep pain, no photophobia, no haloes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: microbial keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of deep pain, photophobia and haloes is the key history that argues against a sight-threatening cause and permits safe attribution to benign conjunctivitis.
Once dangerous mimics are screened out, a benign surface conjunctivitis is supported.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vision blurred only by tearing and clears on wiping; no persistent visual loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: microbial keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vision that clears on wiping away tears indicates the blur is discharge-related, not a true reduction in acuity from corneal or anterior-segment pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Occasional contact-lens wearer, lenses currently removed

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: microbial keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Contact-lens wear in a red eye raises microbial keratitis risk; here lenses are removed and there are no keratitis features, but lens use is retained as a safety-net trigger.

Tender preauricular adenopathy is characteristic of viral (adenoviral) conjunctivitis and is unusual in bacterial or allergic conjunctivitis — but it is a clinician palpation sign, not obtainable over chat.
Preauricular adenopathy is typically absent in simple bacterial conjunctivitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Follicular reaction on the inferior palpebral conjunctiva

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A follicular (rather than papillary) tarsal conjunctival reaction supports a viral aetiology — seen only on lid eversion under magnification, so clinician-only and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fluorescein staining negative (no corneal dendrites, ulceration or abrasion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: microbial keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative fluorescein stain excludes a dendritic (HSV) ulcer and corneal epithelial defect — a definitive clinician investigation that telehealth cannot perform, so it is sealed and the AI must rely on the red-flag history instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visual acuity 6/6 in both eyes once discharge cleared; clear corneas, equal reactive pupils, no ciliary flush

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: microbial keratitis / acute angle-closure glaucoma / anterior uveitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal measured acuity, clear corneas, reactive pupils and absent ciliary flush argue strongly against a sight-threatening cause — reassuring but clinician-measured and therefore sealed from the telehealth AI.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Watery gritty bilateral red eye with sequential involvement, a recent URI and tender preauricular nodes together are strongly specific for adenoviral conjunctivitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person slit-lamp examination with fluorescein staining and visual-acuity testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a classic, red-flag-negative viral conjunctivitis; reserved for red-flag features (pain, photophobia, visual loss, contact-lens keratitis suspicion). Routine urgent referral for slit-lamp here is the over-investigation the case tests. It becomes appropriate only if a safety-net red flag emerges.
Not indicated for routine self-limiting viral conjunctivitis; reserved for hyperacute, severe, neonatal or treatment-resistant cases.
